name: Gambia

colors.symbolism: the sun overhead and the savannas of West Africa
the Gambia River, the geographic and economic spine of the country
the country's forests and agricultural land
unity and peace (the narrow fimbriations between the main bands)

description: Five horizontal stripes — red, white (thin), blue, white (thin), green — with the two narrow white bands serving as fimbriations between the three main colours. Aspect ratio 2:3.
history: Adopted on 18 February 1965, the day The Gambia gained independence from the United Kingdom. Designed by accountant Louis Thomasi, the flag deliberately avoids the Pan-African colour palette and any reference to political parties; the simple horizontal triband with white fimbriations was intended as a non-partisan national symbol. The flag was retained unchanged through the 1970–2017 republic and the 1982–1989 Senegambia confederation period.

features.symbolism: red for the savanna and the sun, blue for the Gambia River that defines the country, green for the land and agriculture; the narrow white fimbriations stand for unity and peace
